questions.text: Who wrote 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote this famous tragedy.

questions.text: '1984' was written by George Orwell.
questions.explanation: George Orwell published '1984' in 1949.

questions.text: Which book won the Booker Prize 2023?
options: A. The Seven Moons of Maali Almeida
questions.explanation: Paul Lynch's 'Prophet Song' won the Booker Prize 2023.

questions.text: Match authors with their famous works:
questions.explanation: These are classic pairings of renowned authors and their masterpieces.

questions.text: What is the longest word in the English language?
questions.explanation: Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis (45 letters) is the longest word in major dictionaries.

questions.text: The word 'queue' has four letters after Q.
questions.explanation: Actually, 'queue' has one letter after Q (just 'u'); the other letters are silent.

questions.text: Which of these are plays by Shakespeare? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: A, B, C, E are Shakespeare plays. 'Wait for Godot' is by Samuel Beckett.

questions.text: Assertion: Poetry uses figurative language. Reason: Metaphors and similes are poetic devices.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct and logically related.

questions.text: Who is the author of 'The Great Gatsby'?
questions.explanation: F. Scott Fitzgerald wrote 'The Great Gatsby' in 1925.

questions.text: The famous line 'To be or not to be' is from Shakespeare's ______.
questions.explanation: This is the opening line of the soliloquy in Hamlet, Act III, Scene 1.

questions.text: Which part of speech is the word 'quickly' in the sentence 'She spoke quickly'?
questions.explanation: 'Quickly' modifies the verb 'spoke', describing how the action was performed. Words ending in '-ly' are typically adverbs.

questions.text: The sentence 'He go to school every day' is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: The correct form should be 'He goes to school every day.' The verb must agree with the third-person singular subject 'he'.

questions.text: Identify the tense in the sentence 'She has been writing a novel for two years.'
questions.explanation: The use of 'has been writing' indicates an action that started in the past and continues to the present, which is Present Perfect Continuous tense.

questions.text: The word 'beautiful' is a ________ because it describes a noun.
questions.explanation: Adjectives are words that modify or describe nouns. 'Beautiful' describes the quality of a noun.

questions.text: Which sentence uses the correct form of the pronoun?
options: A. Me and him are going to the store.
B. Him and I are going to the store.
C. He and I are going to the store.
D. Us are going to the store.
questions.explanation: When pronouns are used as subjects, they should be in the nominative case: 'he' and 'I' are correct, not 'me' or 'him'.

questions.text: Assertion: Articles are essential parts of speech in English. Reason: They help specify whether a noun is definite or indefinite.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Articles (a, an, the) specify whether a noun is definite (the) or indefinite (a/an).

questions.text: Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.
options: A. The team are playing well together.
B. The team is playing well together.
C. The teams is playing well together.
D. The team have been playing well together.
questions.explanation: 'Team' is a singular collective noun, so it requires a singular verb 'is', not the plural 'are'.

questions.text: She is ________ to complete the project by next week.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'able to' is the correct construction for expressing capability. Other options like 'capability' would require different phrasing.

questions.text: The word 'carefully' is an adverb.
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' ends in '-ly' and modifies verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs, making it an adverb.

questions.text: Which sentence is in the passive voice?
options: A. The cat chased the mouse.
B. The mouse was chased by the cat.
C. The cat is chasing the mouse.
D. The mouse chases the cat.
questions.explanation: In passive voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject. 'The mouse was chased by the cat' shows the mouse as the subject receiving the action.

questions.text: Match the tenses with their examples:
pairs: I have lived here for five years
I will be sleeping at 9 PM
I had finished my work before he arrived
questions.explanation: Each example correctly demonstrates the tense structure and temporal reference indicated.

questions.text: Identify the conjunction in the sentence 'I wanted to go, but it was raining.'
questions.explanation: Conjunctions connect clauses or phrases. 'But' is a coordinating conjunction connecting two independent clauses.

questions.text: The phrase 'a unique opportunity' uses the correct article.
questions.explanation: 'A' is used before consonant sounds, and 'unique' begins with a consonant sound, making 'a unique' correct.

questions.text: She is ________ to her work.
questions.explanation: 'Committed' is an adjective that describes dedication or loyalty to something.

questions.text: Which word is a preposition?
questions.explanation: Prepositions show the relationship between nouns/pronouns and other words. 'Between' indicates position or relation.

questions.text: Assertion: 'Neither...nor' is a correlative conjunction. Reason: It connects two negative alternatives in a sentence.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. 'Neither...nor' is a correlative conjunction used to present two negative options.

questions.text: Which of the following sentences are grammatically correct? (Select all correct)
options: B. He go to the gym regularly.
C. They are studying for their exams.
E. You have done excellent work.
questions.explanation: C and E are grammatically correct. A, B, and D have subject-verb agreement errors.

questions.text: Convert the active sentence to passive: 'The baker made fresh bread.'
options: A. Fresh bread made the baker.
B. Fresh bread was made by the baker.
C. Fresh bread is made by baker.
D. The baker had made fresh bread.
questions.explanation: In the passive voice, the object becomes the subject, and the verb form changes to 'was made by' to show the action and agent.

questions.text: The sentence 'Each of the students have submitted their assignment' is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: 'Each' is a singular pronoun, so the verb should be 'has', not 'have'. The correct form is 'has submitted'.

questions.text: The pronoun 'whom' is used as the ________ of a verb or preposition.
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is the objective case of 'who' and functions as the object of a verb or preposition.

questions.text: Which sentence correctly uses a dependent clause?
options: B. Although he studied hard, he failed the exam.
C. She was tired. Because she didn't sleep.
questions.explanation: A dependent clause requires an independent clause to form a complete sentence. Option B correctly combines a dependent clause with an independent clause.

questions.text: Match the words with their parts of speech:
questions.explanation: Each word is correctly classified by its part of speech and function in sentences.

questions.text: Assertion: The word 'their' is a possessive pronoun. Reason: It shows ownership or belonging.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. 'Their' is a possessive pronoun indicating something belongs to 'them'.

questions.text: Identify the correct conditional sentence.
options: A. If I will see him, I will tell him.
B. If I see him, I will tell him.
C. If I would see him, I will tell him.
D. If I would see him, I would tell him.
questions.explanation: In First Conditional (possible future), use present tense in the 'if' clause and future tense in the main clause: 'If I see...I will tell'.

questions.text: The phrase 'an elephant' is correct because 'elephant' begins with a vowel sound.
questions.explanation: 'An' is used before vowel sounds. Even though 'elephant' starts with 'e', the 'eh' sound is a vowel sound.

questions.text: The verb 'run' in the third person singular present tense is ________.
questions.explanation: For third-person singular subjects (he, she, it), add '-s' to regular verbs: run becomes runs.

questions.text: Which sentence demonstrates correct use of reported speech?
options: A. He said that he is coming tomorrow.
B. He said that he was coming the next day.
C. He said he will come tomorrow.
D. He said me that he is coming.
questions.explanation: In reported speech, the tense shifts back (is → was) and 'tomorrow' becomes 'the next day'. Option B correctly demonstrates this.

questions.text: Which of the following use the correct comparative form? (Select all correct)
options: A. This book is more interesting than that one.
B. She is taller than him.
C. He is more clever than his brother.
D. That building is the most highest in the city.
E. She runs faster than her friend.
questions.explanation: A, B, E are correct. C should use 'cleverer' for one-syllable adjectives. D incorrectly uses 'most highest' (superlative with comparative).

questions.text: Assertion: Complex sentences contain at least one dependent clause. Reason: A dependent clause cannot stand alone as a complete sentence.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Complex sentences require independent and dependent clauses, and dependent clauses need independent ones to be complete.

questions.text: Which word is an example of a gerund?
questions.explanation: A gerund is a verb form ending in '-ing' that functions as a noun. 'Running' can be used as a noun (e.g., 'Running is healthy').

questions.text: The phrase 'I wish I ________ more time' requires a past tense verb to express an unreal wish.
questions.explanation: In wishes about the present, use past tense: 'I wish I had more time.' This shows the wish is contrary to reality.

questions.text: The sentence 'Neither of the options are correct' uses proper subject-verb agreement.
questions.explanation: 'Neither' is singular, so it requires a singular verb: 'Neither of the options is correct.'

questions.text: Identify the infinitive in the sentence 'She wants to become a doctor.'
questions.explanation: An infinitive is 'to' + the base form of a verb. 'To become' is the infinitive in this sentence.

questions.text: Match the sentence types with their characteristics:
pairs: Two independent clauses joined by conjunction
Two independent and at least one dependent clause
questions.explanation: Each sentence type is defined by the number and relationship of its clauses.

questions.text: Assertion: Adverbs can modify verbs, adjectives, and other adverbs. Reason: Adverbs are extremely flexible parts of speech.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Adverbs can modify various parts of speech, making them flexible elements in sentences.

questions.text: Choose the correct sentence using the phrasal verb 'look after'.
options: A. She is looking after her younger siblings.
B. She is looking her younger siblings after.
C. She is after looking her younger siblings.
D. She is looking to her younger siblings after.
questions.explanation: 'Look after' means 'to care for'. The correct word order is subject + verb + object, making option A correct.

questions.text: The word 'beautiful' can function as both an adjective and an adverb.
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' is an adjective. The adverbial form would be 'beautifully', which modifies verbs or other adjectives.

questions.text: The collective noun 'audience' is typically treated as a ________ noun.
questions.explanation: Collective nouns (audience, team, family) are usually treated as singular, taking singular verbs.

questions.text: Which sentence correctly uses a semicolon?
options: A. I like tea; but she prefers coffee.
B. She studied hard; therefore, she passed the exam.
C. The weather is sunny; it is warm outside.
D. He enjoys reading; and writing stories.
questions.explanation: A semicolon can connect two independent clauses, often with a transitional word. Option B correctly uses 'therefore' to connect ideas.

questions.text: Which sentences correctly use parallel structure? (Select all correct)
options: A. She likes swimming, cycling, and to run.
B. He enjoys reading books, writing poetry, and playing music.
C. The team is fast, strong, and plays well.
D. I want to travel, to explore, and to learn.
E. The house is old, spacious, and it is comfortable.
questions.explanation: B and D maintain parallel structure. A mixes gerunds and infinitive. C mixes adjectives and verb. E mixes adjectives and clause.

questions.text: Assertion: The Present Perfect tense is used for actions that started in the past and continue to the present. Reason: It connects past actions with the present time.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) indicates actions with relevance to the present.

questions.text: Identify the modal verb in the sentence 'She might attend the meeting tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: Modal verbs (can, could, may, might, must, will, would) express possibility, permission, obligation. 'Might' indicates possibility.

questions.text: The word 'their' should be replaced with ________ in the sentence 'The students left their books at their desks.'
questions.explanation: Both uses of 'their' are correct. 'Their' is the appropriate possessive pronoun for the plural noun 'students'.

questions.text: The past participle of 'go' is 'went'.
questions.explanation: 'Went' is the simple past tense of 'go'. The past participle is 'gone' (used in 'has gone').

questions.text: Which sentence demonstrates correct punctuation of a quotation?
options: A. She said, 'I will be there soon'.
B. She said, "I will be there soon."
C. She said "I will be there soon".
D. She said, "I will be there soon" .
questions.explanation: In American English, periods go inside quotation marks. The comma after 'said' introduces the quote, and the period is inside the quotes.

questions.text: Match the sentence errors with their corrections:
pairs: They is going to the park.
They are going to the park.
questions.explanation: Each error involves incorrect subject-verb agreement that must be corrected for grammatical accuracy.

questions.text: Assertion: An apostrophe is used to show possession. Reason: It indicates that something belongs to someone or something.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Apostrophes show possession (John's book) and contractions (can't).

questions.text: Which word is spelled correctly when forming a plural?
questions.explanation: Words ending in 'f' or 'fe' change to 'ves' (shelf → shelves). Others: child → children, woman → women, potato → potatoes.

questions.text: The word 'unique' should be preceded by the article 'an' because it begins with a vowel letter.
questions.explanation: Although 'unique' starts with 'u', it has a consonant sound (/yu/), so 'a unique' is correct, not 'an unique'.

questions.text: The superlative form of the adjective 'good' is ________.
questions.explanation: 'Good' is an irregular adjective with comparative 'better' and superlative 'best'.

questions.text: How many articles are there in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution has 395 articles (now 470+ after amendments) divided into 22 parts and 12 schedules.

questions.text: The Indian Constitution was adopted on January 26, 1950.
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution came into effect on January 26, 1950, which is celebrated as Republic Day.

questions.text: Which article of the Indian Constitution grants fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12-35 cover Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution, including right to equality, freedom, and constitutional remedies.

questions.text: The power to amend the Constitution lies with the ________ of India.
questions.explanation: The Parliament of India has the exclusive power to amend the Constitution through the amendment process.

questions.text: How many fundamental rights are mentioned in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: There are 6 fundamental rights: Equality, Freedom, against Exploitation, Religion, Education, and Cultural/Property Rights.

questions.text: Assertion: India is a Federal structure. Reason: Power is shared between Central and State governments.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. India is a federal structure with powers divided between Union and State governments.

questions.text: The Prime Minister of India is directly elected by the people.
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is elected by the Parliament (Lok Sabha), not directly by the people. The President is the head of state.

questions.text: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36-51) of the Constitution contains Directive Principles of State Policy, which are guidelines for state governance.

questions.text: The term of the President of India is ________ years.
questions.explanation: The President's term is 5 years, and they can be re-elected for a maximum of 2 terms.

questions.text: Which of the following are organs of the Indian Government? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The three organs of government are Legislative, Executive, and Judiciary. Military and Media are important but not constitutional organs.

questions.text: How many members are there in the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha (Upper House) has 250 members: 238 elected from states and 12 nominated by the President.

questions.text: Assertion: The Lok Sabha is the lower house of Parliament. Reason: It has more members than the Rajya Sabha.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Lok Sabha (545 members) is the lower house and has more members than Rajya Sabha (250).

questions.text: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by the Prime Minister.
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by the President on the advice of senior judges, not directly by the PM.

questions.text: Match the constitutional positions with their tenure:
questions.explanation: Each position has specific tenure rules under the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: Which constitutional amendment introduced the concept of 'Right to Information'?
options: D. It's a separate Act, not from Constitution
questions.explanation: Right to Information is not from a constitutional amendment but from the Right to Information Act, 2005.

questions.text: The minimum age to become a voter in India is ________ years.
questions.explanation: Citizens of India who are 18 years or older have the right to vote in elections.

questions.text: Which is the highest court in India?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India is the highest judicial authority in the country.

questions.text: The Vice President of India presides over the Rajya Sabha.
questions.explanation: The Vice President is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha and chairs its proceedings.

questions.text: Assertion: The Speaker of Lok Sabha is elected by Lok Sabha members. Reason: The Speaker is the presiding officer of the Lok Sabha.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Speaker is elected by Lok Sabha members and presides over its proceedings.

questions.text: Which of the following are Fundamental Duties under the Indian Constitution? (Select all correct)
options: B. To promote harmony and fraternity
questions.explanation: A, B, C, E are Fundamental Duties (Articles 51A). D is not a constitutional duty.

questions.text: The term 'secular' was added to the Preamble through the ________ Constitutional Amendment.
questions.explanation: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment (1976) made the Constitution more secular by adding 'secular' to the Preamble.

questions.text: How many schedules are there in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution has 12 schedules listing matters like official languages, states, and powers of government.

questions.text: Assertion: Elections in India are conducted by the Election Commission. Reason: The Commission ensures free and fair elections.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Election Commission of India is responsible for conducting and supervising elections.

questions.text: The minimum age to become President of India is 35 years.
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 35 years old and an Indian citizen to become President of India.

questions.text: Match the constitutional articles with their subjects:
questions.explanation: These are landmark articles dealing with fundamental aspects of the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: How many days does Parliament get to pass a Money Bill after receiving it from the President?
questions.explanation: The President has 14 days to return a Money Bill or it becomes law automatically after 30 days of receipt by Parliament.

questions.text: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution begins with the words 'We, the ________'.
questions.explanation: The Preamble starts with 'We, the People of India' establishing popular sovereignty.

questions.text: The Constitution can be amended by a simple majority in Parliament.
questions.explanation: Most constitutional amendments require a 2/3 majority in both houses; some require ratification by states.

questions.text: Which article abolishes untouchability in India?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Constitution abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice.

questions.text: Assertion: The Cabinet is responsible to the Lok Sabha. Reason: The Prime Minister is accountable to Parliament.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Indian government follows parliamentary democracy where Cabinet is responsible to Lok Sabha.

questions.text: Which of the following are qualifications to become a Member of Parliament? (Select all correct)
options: B. At least 25 years old for Lok Sabha
C. At least 30 years old for Rajya Sabha
D. Must own property worth minimum amount
questions.explanation: A, B, C are correct qualifications. D and E are not constitutional requirements.

questions.text: The right to ________ is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
questions.explanation: Article 21 guarantees the right to life and personal liberty to all citizens.

questions.text: How many Union Territories are there in India as of 2024?
questions.explanation: As of 2024, there are 8 Union Territories: Delhi, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Dadra & Nagar Haveli, Daman & Diu, Lakshadweep, Puducherry, Ladakh.

questions.text: Assertion: The Constitution grants freedom of speech and expression. Reason: This right can be restricted on grounds of national security.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Article 19 grants freedom with reasonable restrictions.

questions.text: The Governor of a state is appointed by the Prime Minister.
questions.explanation: The Governor is appointed by the President on the advice of the Union Government, not the PM directly.

questions.text: Match the government bodies with their functions:
questions.explanation: Each body has distinct constitutional functions in the Indian governance structure.

questions.text: Which article provides the right to equality before law in India?
questions.explanation: Article 14 ensures equality before law and equal protection to all persons.

questions.text: The Cabinet of Ministers is headed by the ________.
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Cabinet and the chief executive of the government.

questions.text: The Constitution recognizes caste, religion, or gender as grounds for discrimination under Article 15.
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth.

questions.text: How many Vice Presidents can India have at the same time?
questions.explanation: India has only one Vice President at a time, who serves for a 5-year term.

questions.text: Assertion: The Chief Minister is the head of a state government. Reason: The Chief Minister is the leader of the majority in the state assembly.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The Chief Minister is the chief executive of the state and leads the government.

questions.text: Which of the following fall under the Concurrent List? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Education, taxation, and forests are on the Concurrent List. Police and Defence are Union subjects.

questions.text: The power to dissolve the Lok Sabha lies with the ________.
questions.explanation: The President has the power to dissolve the Lok Sabha on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.text: All bills passed by Parliament require the President's assent to become law.
questions.explanation: Bills passed by Parliament must receive the President's assent before becoming law.

questions.text: Which constitutional amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment (1989) lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.text: Match the lists in the Constitution with their subjects:
questions.explanation: The Constitution divides powers between Union and States through these three lists.

questions.text: Assertion: India practices universal adult suffrage. Reason: Every adult citizen has the right to vote.
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. Universal adult suffrage means every citizen 18+ has voting rights.

questions.text: The oath of office for the President is administered by the ________.
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India administers the oath of office to the President of India.

questions.text: The Constitution recognizes English as an official language of India.
questions.explanation: While Hindi is the official language, English continues as an associate official language for government functions.

questions.text: Which article of the Constitution provides for the abolition of slavery?
questions.explanation: Article 18 abolishes titles and prevents the state from conferring titles except military and academic ones.
